blocks.text: The battery sensor is used to measure the current, voltage, and temperature flowing in the 12V battery and is mounted between the negative terminal of the 12V battery and body ground. The values measured by the battery sensor are sent to the PGM-FI ECU using LIN upon request from the PGM-FI ECU. The battery sensor has an internal self-diagnostic function, and the self-diagnostic results are sent to the PGM-FI ECU using LIN upon request from the PGM-FI ECU. If the self-diagnostic result sent from the battery sensor is abnormal, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: If any of the following conditions persists for more than 5 seconds

items.text: Disconnection of current detection circuit, abnormality of A/D conversion circuit.
Disconnection or short circuit in the voltage detection circuit, or abnormality in the A/D conversion circuit.
Disconnection or short circuit of the temperature detection circuit, or abnormality of the A/D conversion circuit.
Failure of 12V battery internal resistance measurement circuit or power supply system
Battery Disconnected sensor power supply line or blown fuse

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
